text: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit - BBC
fullText: Tehran says the US strikes hit bridges, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

text: Japan revises law to ensure supply of (male) heirs to the imperial throne - NPR
fullText: A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.

text: A Pitt professor breaks down what to know about the diarrhea-causing cyclosporiasis - Pittsburgh Post-Gazette
‘God of War’ Shocker: Kratos Actor Ryan Hurst Recast After On-Set Injury - Variety
fullText: Prime Video's "God of War" adaptation has recast the lead role of Kratos after star Ryan Hurst suffered an injury on set.

text: At least 2 dead, hundreds rescued as flooding devastates parts of Texas - Austin American-Statesman
fullText: At least two people died and hundreds were rescued Thursday as dangerous flooding swept across the Texas Hill Country.

text: After Blue Angels low flyover, acting Navy secretary says: ‘No firings’ - The Washington Post
fullText: The flyover, which the Blue Angels said was lower than standard, sent umbrellas and chairs toppling. The acting Navy secretary later posted “That’s the sound of Freedom!”

text: Xi Jinping spearheads China’s AI push - Financial Times
fullText: President calls for ‘positive’ use of technology as Beijing seeks to strengthen influence over standards

text: Portland sends first term sheet to Trail Blazers days after NBA says talks 'off track' - KATU
fullText: In what’s perhaps a surprising development, city leaders have sent an initial term sheet to the Trail Blazers this week outlining the city’s first proposal in negotiating a long-term agreement to keep the team.

text: Zelensky ousts popular defense minister, an architect of Ukraine’s drone program - The Washington Post
fullText: Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal, part of a rebooting of the government announced Sunday, prompted protests in Kyiv.

text: AP Exclusive: ICE officer in Maine shooting has history of violent behavior, family and records say - AP News
fullText: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, according to close relatives who spoke to The Associated Press. Several o…

text: CNBC Daily Open: Trump takes the stage — without his longtime teleprompter operator - CNBC
fullText: U.S. President Donald Trump is poised to give a primetime speech on Thursday focusing on election security, although with a new teleprompter operator.

text: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt - SpaceNews
fullText: SpaceX aborted the launch of its latest Starship test flight July 16 when some of the vehicle’s engines failed to ignite.

text: Exclusive | Bill Essayli calls for investigation after rare cancer diagnoses rattle Orange County families - New York Post
fullText: An outbreak of an extremely rare cancer in a ritzy SoCal community has raised the concern of Los Angeles’ top federal attorney.

text: Kit Connor and Joe Locke: 'Making a film like Heartstopper will forever be important' - BBC
fullText: The Netflix movie brings Alice Oseman's popular screen adaptation to a conclusion.

text: As wildfire smoke makes air unhealthy from Midwest to East Coast, officials say stay inside - PBS
fullText: Heavy, pungent wildfire smoke darkened skies in the U.S. on Thursday from the Great Lakes to parts of the East Coast, reducing visibility and prompting warnings that breathing the air outside could be dangerous.

text: The NBA Wants to Set Next Season’s TV Schedule, But It’s Waiting On LeBron - The Hollywood Reporter
fullText: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver said that the league does not want to schedule next season's games until it knows where the (now former) Lakers star lands.

text: Ebola spreading more quickly in DRC, while Uganda is close to being virus-free - Al Jazeera
fullText: Uganda ⁠⁠discharges last-remaining patient, as WHO says Ebola has ‘expanded faster than any previous outbreak’ in DRC.

text: Netflix Stock Drops on Revenue Miss, Engagement Update - Barron's
fullText: Revenue for the quarter of $12.56 billion was below the $12.58 billion analysts were expecting.
